PV0-G01.reason: Repository inputs now have immutable commits, blob object IDs, and SHA-256 digests. Public-standard terms and provenance-owner approval remain outstanding.

PV0-G02.reason: RED inputs and prohibited uses are explicit, but the current repository-exposed authoring history is not clean-room evidence.

PV0-G03.reason: The requirement catalogue was reconstructed in the same commit as the schema draft.

PV0-G04.reason: The incubator schemas remain review-only and were authored with repository exposure.

requirementsCatalogue.warning: The catalogue was recorded in the same change as the schemas and does not prove that approved requirements preceded authoring.

contextClasses: public standards listed in the protocol README
review findings produced during the same task
aiAssistance.disclosure: Repository exposure means this incubator draft is not accepted as clean-room evidence. No permissive relicensing, SDK generation, publication, or private implementation use is authorized.

candidateInputs.title: CLODEx Open/Closed Boundary Policy
candidateInputs.copyrightAndAuthorEvidence: Git commit metadata records SSY <mereyabdenbekuly@gmail.com>; final ownership and contributor-rights review remains external.
candidateInputs.licenseOrTerms: Current repository license; no relicensing conclusion.
candidateInputs.permittedUse: High-level public/private product boundary, schema-only authorization, explicit exclusions, and gate ordering.
candidateInputs.prohibitedUse: Using policy prose as authority to copy or relicense implementation source.
candidateInputs.blocker: Provenance owner must approve the exact stated use.

candidateInputs.title: OpenAPI Specification 3.2.0
candidateInputs.permittedUse: OpenAPI document structure and dialect declaration.
candidateInputs.prohibitedUse: Copying non-permitted prose, examples, or reference implementation code.
candidateInputs.blocker: Record terms/notice review and provenance-owner approval.

observedTermsEvidence.observation: Official versioned specification source for OAS 3.2.0.

candidateInputs.title: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
candidateInputs.prohibitedUse: Treating a schema vocabulary as approval of CLODEx-specific field structure.

observedTermsEvidence.observation: Official repository states BSD-3-Clause or Academic Free License 3.0; exact choice and notice treatment remain reviewer decisions.

candidateInputs.title: RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme
candidateInputs.prohibitedUse: Copying reference code without a separate license review.
candidateInputs.blocker: Record terms/notice review and provenance-owner approval.

observedTermsEvidence.observation: Official RFC Editor record invokes IETF Trust legal provisions; RFC 8785 is an Independent Submission, so text/code reuse treatment remains review-pending.
Current IETF Trust legal-provisions reference; no RFC prose or reference code is copied into the incubator.

observedTermsEvidence.observation: Official RFC Editor record identifies the 2006 standards-track document and its legacy copying conditions; only interoperability requirements are cited.

candidateInputs.title: CLODEx public implementation repository
candidateInputs.permittedUse: Component/provenance inventory, negative dependency constraints, and black-box behavior only when separately approved.
candidateInputs.prohibitedUse: Copying, translating, structurally following, or generating schemas, SDK types, validators, conformance vectors, tests, or private code from implementation source.
candidateInputs.blocker: A separate independently constrained author/reviewer context is required before fresh schema or vector authorship can close PV0-G02, PV0-G04, or PV0-G06.

excludedInputClasses: private threat intelligence, benchmark holdouts, or anti-abuse heuristics
current implementation types, validators, tests, fixtures, constants, comments, distinctive literals, or module structure as a blueprint
Stagewise-derived agent-shell, agent-core, Karton, browser IPC, UI, or runtime source
runner-sdk source or exports while it depends on agent-shell
other AI answers as legal authority or normative protocol authority
private repository material, production topology, credentials, or signing identifiers

repositoryExposedAgentsMay: validate the incubator draft for internal consistency
repositoryExposedAgentsMustNot: implement or seed a private Gateway
